lessons.title: My Job and Daily Routine
lessons.system_prompt: Teach the student to describe their job: 'I am a [job]', 'I work at [place]', 'I start work at [time]', 'My job is [adjective]'. Use present simple tense only. American variant: casual, upbeat tone.

steps.teacher_prompt: Hey! Today we're going to talk about jobs. Tell me — what do you do for work? Try using: 'I am a...' Go for it!
steps.expected_student_response: Student says their job using 'I am a [job]'

steps.teacher_prompt: Cool! Now let's say where you work. You say: 'I work at [place]'. For example: 'I work at a hospital' or 'I work at home'. Where do you work?
steps.expected_student_response: Student says their workplace using 'I work at [place]'

steps.teacher_prompt: Nice! Let's talk about your schedule. Say: 'I start work at [time]'. For example: 'I start work at nine o'clock.' What time do you start work?
steps.expected_student_response: Student says their start time using 'I start work at [time]'

steps.teacher_prompt: You're crushing it! Now tell me everything about your job — what you do, where you work, and when you start. Make it sound natural!
steps.expected_student_response: Student gives a full description of their job routine

steps.teacher_prompt: You did fantastic today! Now you can talk about your job in English. Remember: 'I am a...', 'I work at...', 'I start at...'. Great work — see you next time!

lessons.title: My Job and Daily Routine
lessons.system_prompt: Teach the student to describe their job: 'I am a [job]', 'I work at [place]', 'I start work at [time]', 'My job is [adjective]'. Use present simple tense only. British variant: professional, measured tone.

steps.teacher_prompt: Good day! Today we shall talk about occupations. Could you tell me what you do for a living? Try saying: 'I am a...' — off you go!
steps.expected_student_response: Student says their job using 'I am a [job]'

steps.teacher_prompt: Very good! Now let's say where you are employed. The phrase is: 'I work at [place]'. For example: 'I work at a school' or 'I work at an office'. Where do you work?
steps.expected_student_response: Student says their workplace using 'I work at [place]'

steps.teacher_prompt: Splendid! Now, what time do you begin work? Say: 'I start work at [time]'. For instance: 'I start work at half past eight.' What time do you start?
steps.expected_student_response: Student says their start time using 'I start work at [time]'

steps.teacher_prompt: Jolly good! Now could you describe your working life — your job, your workplace, and when you commence work? Take your time and speak naturally.
steps.expected_student_response: Student gives a full description of their job routine

steps.teacher_prompt: Well done indeed! You can now describe your work in English. Remember the key phrases: 'I am a...', 'I work at...', 'I start work at...'. Carry on practising — you're doing brilliantly!
